dimensions.note: Docs-only change (module/model JSDoc + README + version bump); no credential-handling code touched. Credentials still come from the environment (ambient chain, fromIni, fromTemporaryCredentials for AssumeRole); nothing vaulted or stored. No secret was introduced into the corrected doc text — the added permission string lambda:DeleteFunction is an IAM action name, not a secret.

No logging code changed. The structured-placeholder logging in processStack (predelete/pass1 messages) already existed and is unchanged; this change only aligns the prose docs to that existing behavior.

No error-handling code changed. The documented fatal-vs-skip split and narrow catches (ValidationError, ResourceNotFoundException on DeleteFunction, NoSuchEntity on GetRole) are untouched; the doc rewrite makes the DeleteFunction path visible in prose, matching the existing catch of ResourceNotFoundException around api.deleteFunction.

No behavior change, so no new tests required. Ran the full suite for the touched dir: 49 passed / 0 failed, including the safeNameSegment slugify/90-char-cap and orphanKey sanitization tests that back the newly-documented README key note, and the manifest-name/version-sync + upgrade-chain-invariant tests that cover the version bump.

No behavior change. The corrected module doc now accurately describes the default predelete-Lambda-then-plain-DeleteStack path (predeleteLambda default true, verified at the arguments schema) and the predeleteLambda=false two-pass fallback, which is what processStack already does; documenting the real path improves operator reproducibility of a re-runnable cleanup.

No API-calling code changed. The doc now names lambda:DeleteFunction as a required action alongside cloudformation:DeleteStack and iam:GetRole, matching the actual DeleteFunctionCommand/DeleteStackCommand/GetRoleCommand calls in the source — the module doc previously omitted DeleteFunction, so this brings the doc into contract with the code.
No resource-lifecycle code changed. Per-region memoized clients and abort-signal threading are untouched by this docs-only edit.

Verified the edited surfaces: module-doc cleanup bullet now leads with the predelete-Lambda default and lists cloudformation:DeleteStack + lambda:DeleteFunction + iam:GetRole; model-export JSDoc aligned to the same framing; README orphan/deletion key sections and the CEL-query section now note safeNameSegment sanitization; the illustrative cleanup workflow step now shows args: { apply: true } with a comment matching the dry-run default. No real account ids/hostnames introduced (README examples remain synthetic repeated-digit placeholders). Manifest version and model.version aligned at 2026.07.19.0.

No zod schema changed by this docs-only edit. The orphan/summary/deletion/org-summary schemas and their deliberate .passthrough() (forward-compat, documented on org-summary) still hold; schema-write conformance is unaffected and remains as verified at first publish.
No lifetime/GC settings changed. Every spec retains lifetime infinite with garbageCollection 10, matching the accumulate-and-query design where rows are keyed per account/region/stack.

This is a mutating model whose sole state-changing operation is deletion; that path is complete and guarded (single-account cleanup + org-wide cleanupOrg, both dry-run by default, name-prefix scoped, custom-resource-only retain). It intentionally offers no create/update because it retires externally-created StackSet-orphan stacks it never provisions. The corrected docs now describe the full delete path (predelete-Lambda default plus two-pass fallback) rather than only the retain-delete variant, so the documented delete surface matches the implementation. No code changed.

No guard code changed. The mutating-path guards (apply defaults to false, expectAccount throws before any write, per-member landing check, namePrefix refusal, computeRetain custom-resource-only retain) still hold, and the corrected docs continue to describe them accurately including the refuses-names-outside-namePrefix clause.

The README now correctly documents that the orphan-/deletion- key <stack> segment is passed through safeNameSegment (unsafe-char runs collapsed to '-', truncated to 90 chars), matching orphanKey/deletion-key construction in source (safeNameSegment at line 354, applied at the orphanKey and deletion-key builders). This closes the doc gap where an exact-key CEL lookup on an unsafe/over-cap name would have missed.
No data-access code changed. cleanup still re-enumerates targets live rather than reading stored rows; the doc edit does not alter this.

Invariant holds: model.version bumped to 2026.07.19.0 and a final upgrades entry appended with toVersion 2026.07.19.0 (equal to model.version) whose upgradeAttributes passes old attributes through unchanged — a no-op consistent with a docs-only change and no resource schema changes. The colocated chain-ends-at-model.version test passes.
